report_name: Tennessee Hospital Notifies 337,000 Patients of Data Breach, Nine Months After Rhysida Ransomware Attack

threat_actors.name: Rhysida
threat_actors.description: A ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) group that conducted a double-extortion attack against Cookeville Regional Medical Center in July 2025. The group claimed the attack on its dark web leak site, stating it stole 500GB of data.
threat_actors.goals: To extort money from victims by encrypting their data and threatening to leak stolen sensitive information.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The Rhysida group exfiltrated 500GB of sensitive data from the hospital's network as part of their double-extortion strategy.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Rhysida ransomware was used to encrypt systems within the Cookeville Regional Medical Center network, disrupting operations and holding data hostage.

malware.name: Rhysida Ransomware
malware.description: A ransomware payload deployed by the Rhysida group to encrypt files on the victim's network. It is used in double-extortion attacks where data is first exfiltrated.

identities.name: Cookeville Regional Medical Center (CRMC)
identities.description: A medical center in Tennessee that was the victim of a Rhysida ransomware attack in July 2025, resulting in a data breach affecting 337,917 individuals.

infrastructure.name: Rhysida Dark Web Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by the Rhysida group to publicly name their victims, advertise stolen data for sale, and eventually leak the data if no ransom is paid.

indicators.name: Rhysida Ransom Note IOC
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Rhysida Ransom Note File Creation
id: 2b1a9c8d-4e5f-4a3b-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of the Rhysida ransom note file 'CriticalBreachDetected.pdf'.
references:
  - https://cybernews.com/news/337k-exposed-rhysida-ransomware-attack-tennessee-cookeville-regional-medical-center/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-20
tags:
  - attack.t1486
level: critical
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\CriticalBreachDetected.pdf'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of a file named 'CriticalBreachDetected.pdf', which is the ransom note dropped by Rhysida ransomware.

indicators.name: Rhysida Encrypted File Detection
indicators.pattern:
title: Rhysida Encrypted File Extension
id: 5d8c3b1a-9e8f-4c7d-8a6b-2c1d4e5f6a3b
status: experimental
description: Detects files being created or renamed with the .rhysida extension, indicating Rhysida ransomware activity.
references:
  - https://cybernews.com/news/337k-exposed-rhysida-ransomware-attack-tennessee-cookeville-regional-medical-center/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-20
tags:
  - attack.t1486
level: critical
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.rhysida'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects files being encrypted by Rhysida ransomware, identified by the unique '.rhysida' file extension appended to filenames.

indicators.name: Anomalous Large Outbound Data Transfer
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Large Outbound Data Transfer
id: 9a8b7c6d-5e4f-3a2b-1c0d-9e8f7a6b5c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects potentially anomalous large outbound data transfers from internal servers, which could indicate data exfiltration prior to a ransomware attack.
references:
  - https://cybernews.com/news/337k-exposed-rhysida-ransomware-attack-tennessee-cookeville-regional-medical-center/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-20
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate large data transfers, such as database backups or software distribution.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: firewall
detection:
  selection:
    action: 'allow'
    sent_bytes|gt: 100000000 # Example threshold: 100MB
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects anomalous large outbound data transfers from internal servers, a potential indicator of data exfiltration as seen in the Rhysida attack.
